statement: SK hynix, Samsung, and Micron have begun full-scale development of 16-Hi HBM4 memory for NVIDIA, targeting delivery by Q4 2026 — a forward supply-chain ramp for NVIDIA's next-gen accelerators.
why: NVIDIA's request for 16-Hi HBM4 by Q4 2026 plus three suppliers' full-scale development work indicate HBM capacity being built to meet next-gen GPU demand.

impact.mechanism: Multi-vendor 16-Hi HBM4 development expands high-bandwidth memory supply for NVIDIA's next-generation GPUs.

evidence.source: TweakTown — SK hynix/Samsung/Micron fight for NVIDIA 16-Hi HBM4 orders
evidence.excerpt: NVIDIA requested that domestic and foreign memory manufacturers deliver 16-Hi HBM memory chips by Q4 2026.

evidence.source: TweakTown — SK hynix/Samsung/Micron fight for NVIDIA 16-Hi HBM4 orders
evidence.excerpt: SK hynix and Samsung Electronics in South Korea, as well as US-based Micron, have all begun full-scale development work for the mass production supply of 16-Hi HBM memory chips to NVIDIA.

confidence.basis: single trade-press source, development-stage (no production volumes)
